message.content:
I'm planning to add server-side pagination + server-side filtering/sorting to the RFQ list query in C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend. I need to understand the current query and what is DB-backed vs computed.

Explore C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend and report:

1. **Customer.getAll** in models/lib.model.js (around line 340-482): the full function. What request fields it reads (req.body.status, divisionCode, divisionStatus, highlightedItem, filterd_rfq), the where `condition` it builds (vendor vs internal role), `order`, `subQuery`, and the findAll with `attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES }` and `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(...)`. Quote it with line numbers.

2. **The include builders**: find and quote `buildRfqVendorsInclude` and `buildRfqListLookupIncludes` (and RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES). What associations are eager-loaded (RFQVendors, Factory, merch group, etc.)? This matters for whether pagination with includes needs subQuery/distinct.

3. **utility.remapRFQItem** in helpers/utility.js: this transforms the Sequelize rows into the client shape. Critically, enumerate which output fields are (a) direct copies of DB columns, vs (b) COMPUTED in JS (e.g. 'FOB Cost', 'Landed Cost', 'CBM/pc', 'Price for Sales', margins, merch-group names, vendor info). Quote the computed ones. I need to know which grid columns map to a real DB column (sortable/filterable in SQL) vs computed-in-JS (cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL without replicating logic).

4. **The RFQItem model**: in models/db/RFQItem.js, list the actual DB columns (names). Cross-reference: for the key grid columns (Price Code, Description, Status, Date Modified, RFQ Group, Customer, dates, prices), what are the underlying DB column names? Note which "computed" grid values actually ARE persisted as columns (e.g. is landed cost stored as rfqItem_... ? earlier I saw rfqItem_is_landed_cost_manual).

5. **Routing**: how /getAllItem maps to Customer.getAll (routes/lib.router.js), and whether the BFF (designflow-bff SERVICE_MAP) routes /api/core/* to this backend.

6. Any existing pagination/sort/filter translation helper anywhere in designflow-backend I could reuse (search for startRow, endRow, sortModel, filterModel, limit, offset).

Report concrete code with file:line references relative to C:\repos\dflow. Be exhaustive on #2, #3, #4 — the computed-vs-DB-column distinction is the crux. Return only findings, no preamble.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: I'll systematically explore the designflow-backend codebase to understand the current RFQ list query structure and identify computed vs DB-backed fields.

designflow-backend\helpers\utility.js
designflow-backend\models\db\RFQItem.js
designflow-backend\routes\lib.router.js

1	var DBConfig = require('../config/db.config')()
2	var Sequelize = require('sequelize');
3	var db = {};
4	
5	initialize();
6	async function initialize() {
7	    // connect to db
8	    let sq_config = {
9	        dialect: 'postgres',
10	        host: DBConfig.HOST,
11	        schema: process.env.SCHEMA,
12	        logging: false,
13	        pool: {
14	            max: 22,
15	            min: 1,
16	            acquire: 600000,
17	            idle: 100000,
18	        },
19	        createdAt: false,
20	        updatedAt: false,
21	        timestamp: false,
22	        dialectOptions: {
23	            connectTimeout: 3000000,
24	            // Force UTF-8 on the wire so multibyte text (e.g. Chinese headers)
25	            // can never be reinterpreted through a non-UTF-8 codepage. Defense
26	            // against the designflow_dev CP437 mojibake incident (see AGENTS.md).
27	            client_encoding: 'UTF8',
28	        }
29	    };
30	
31	    const sequelize = new Sequelize(DBConfig.DB, DBConfig.DBUser, DBConfig.PASSWORD, sq_config);
32	    try {
33	        sequelize.authenticate();
34	        console.log('Connection has been established successfully.');
35	    } catch (error) {
36	        console.error('Unable to connect to the database:', error);
37	        throw 'Unable to connect to the database:', error
38	    }
39	
40	    db =  require('./db/init-models')(sequelize);
41	
42	    sequelize.sync();
43	
44	    // Safe one-time migrations: ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS never fails on re-deploy
45	    const schema = process.env.SCHEMA ? `"${process.env.SCHEMA}".` : '';
46	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQItem" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_duty_rate_equation" VARCHAR(500)`)
47	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration rfqItem_duty_rate_equation skipped:', err.message));
48	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"customers" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS customers_logo VARCHAR(500)`)
49	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration customers_logo skipped:', err.message));
50	    sequelize.query(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${schema}app_settings (key VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW())`)
51	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration app_settings skipped:', err.message));
52	    // AI cache telemetry events — append-only per backend response. Admin AI Settings
53	    // aggregates this table to show whether prompt/context caching is actually paying off.
54	    sequelize.query(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${schema}ai_cache_events (
55	      id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
56	      feature VARCHAR(80) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'unknown',
57	      provider VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
58	      api_backend VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
59	      model VARCHAR(160) NOT NULL,
60	      cache_strategy VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
61	      session_id VARCHAR(160),
62	      telemetry_available BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
63	      prompt_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	      cache_hit_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
65	      cache_miss_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
66	      cache_creation_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
67	      completion_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
68	      reasoning_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
69	      total_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
70	      cache_hit_rate_pct DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
71	      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
72	    )`).catch(err => console.warn('Migration ai_cache_events skipped:', err.message));
73	    sequelize.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ai_cache_events_created_at_idx ON ${schema}ai_cache_events (created_at DESC)`)
74	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration ai_cache_events_created_at_idx skipped:', err.message));
75	    sequelize.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ai_cache_events_feature_created_at_idx ON ${schema}ai_cache_events (feature, created_at DESC)`)
76	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration ai_cache_events_feature_created_at_idx skipped:', err.message));
77	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"users" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS profile_photo TEXT`)
78	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration users.profile_photo skipped:', err.message));
79	    // users.graph_photo — cached M365/Teams profile photo URL (DO Spaces) pulled
80	    // from Microsoft Graph. Distinct from profile_photo so a user-uploaded photo
81	    // always takes precedence. graph_photo_synced_at throttles re-fetching (and is
82	    // stamped even when the user has no Graph photo, to avoid hammering Graph).
83	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"users" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS graph_photo TEXT`)
84	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration users.graph_photo skipped:', err.message));
85	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"users" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS graph_photo_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ`)
86	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration users.graph_photo_synced_at skipped:', err.message));
87	    // vendor.vendor_profile_photo — factory (vendor) users live in the vendor table,
88	    // not users, so /api/user/me and /api/user/photo read/store their avatar here.
89	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"vendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS vendor_profile_photo TEXT`)
90	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration vendor.vendor_profile_photo skipped:', err.message));
91	    // RFQItem.price_sales_snapshots — per-row JSON map of price_sales colId ->
92	    // {duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}, snapshotting the cost inputs at
93	    // the moment each "Price for Sales" cell was last populated (shown as a cell tooltip).
94	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQItem" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`)
95	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots skipped:', err.message));
96	    // RFQItem.factories_step_at — timestamp for when an item moves to @factories.
97	    // The mixed-case identifier must be quoted to match Sequelize's generated SQL.
98	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQItem" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_factories_step_at" TIMESTAMPTZ`)
99	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration rfqItem_factories_step_at skipped:', err.message));
100	    // Clean up the empty lowercase orphan column an earlier UNQUOTED version of the
101	    // migration above accidentally created (Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to
102	    // lowercase). It is distinct from the correct mixed-case column and unused.
103	    // Quoted-lowercase DROP targets only the orphan; safe + idempotent via IF EXISTS.
104	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQItem" DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots"`)
105	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration drop orphan rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots skipped:', err.message));
106	    // RFQVendor.requote_requested — true when Sourcing has asked the factory to
107	    // refresh a stale quote. Drives the orange highlight + "Quoted <date>" tooltip
108	    // in the RFQ detail grid; cleared when the vendor re-quotes or clicks "Keep Price".
109	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "requote_requested" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`)
110	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration RFQVendor.requote_requested skipped:', err.message));
111	    // RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived — PER-VENDOR archive flag. Set by the lazy sweep
112	    // in Customer.getAll (helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js) for a factory's own quote
113	    // rows that were priced and whose quote_date is >3 months old. Drives the vendor's
114	    // archive/active tab split independently of the global RFQItem.rfqItem_archive flag,
115	    // so one vendor archiving a stale quote never hides the item from Sourcing or other
116	    // factories. Mixed-case identifier MUST be quoted (see §11 / SYS-002).
117	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "RFQVendor_archived" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`)
118	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived skipped:', err.message));
119	    // RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archive_optout — sticky "vendor manually un-archived this"
120	    // marker. The lazy sweep skips opted-out rows so a manual un-archive is not undone
121	    // on the next list load. Cleared whenever the quote cycle resets (fresh price,
122	    // keepPrice, or a re-quote request), so the 3-month auto-archive can apply again.
123	    // Mixed-case identifier MUST be quoted (see §11 / SYS-002).
124	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "RFQVendor_archive_optout" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`)
125	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archive_optout skipped:', err.message));
126	    // Factory.factory_country — country where the factory operates (FOB origin).
127	    // Used to auto-fill FOB Country on quote rows and to match the Trump 2025
128	    // reciprocal tariff rate when calculating duty.
129	    sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"Factory" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS factory_country VARCHAR(255)`)
130	      .catch(err => console.warn('Migration Factory.factory_country skipped:', err.message));
131	    // One-time backfill: set factory_country from the most common vendor_country
132	    // among vendors linked to each factory. Only fills NULLs so it is safe to re-run.
133	    sequelize.query(`
134	      UPDATE ${schema}"Factory" f
135	      SET factory_country = sub.country
136	      FROM (
137	        SELECT factory_id_fk, vendor_country AS country,
138	               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY factory_id_fk ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rn
139	        FROM ${schema}vendor
140	        WHERE factory_id_fk IS NOT NULL AND vendor_country IS NOT NULL AND vendor_country <> ''
141	        GROUP BY factory_id_fk, vendor_country
142	      ) sub
143	      WHERE sub.rn = 1 AND sub.factory_id_fk = f.id
144	        AND (f.factory_country IS NULL OR f.factory_country = '')
145	    `).catch(err => console.warn('Backfill Factory.factory_country skipped:', err.message));
146	    // grid_cell_notes — per-cell AG Grid notes keyed by grid_type + row_id + col_id.
147	    // Reusable across RFQ, Item Library, and License Tracking grids.
148	    sequelize.query(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${schema}grid_cell_notes (
149	      id UUID PRIMARY KEY,

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

---

## FINDINGS: RFQ List Query Analysis

### 1. Customer.getAll Function (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\models\lib.model.js, lines 347-482)

```javascript
Customer.getAll = async (req, result) => {
  let token = req.headers.authorization;
  let user = await getUserInfo(token)
  let status;
  let condition;
  let order;
  let divisionCodeArray = ['Lic,Gen', 'Gen,Lic'];
  let divisionStatusArray = [];
  let subQuery = false;
  
  // Reads req.body fields:
  if (req.body.status == 'archive') {
    status = 1
  } else {
    status = null
  }
  for (var i = 0; req.body.divisionCode.length > i; i++) {
    divisionCodeArray.push(req.body.divisionCode[i].divCode_code)
  }
  for (var i = 0; req.body.divisionStatus.length > i; i++) {
    divisionStatusArray.push(req.body.divisionStatus[i].RFQItemStatus_id)
  }
  
  // VENDOR ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 367-428)
  if (user.role == 'vendor') {
    // ... vendor filter logic ...
    if (req.body.filterd_rfq == 'true') {
      // Allow showing highlighted item OR items assigned to vendor/factory
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        [Op.or]: [
          { '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter },
          { 'rfqItem_style_number': req.body.highlightedItem }
        ]
      }
    } else {
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter
      }
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"RFQVendor_id" DESC')]
  } else {
    // INTERNAL ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 432-446)
    condition = {
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$': status,
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"rfqItem_id" DESC')]
    subQuery = true
  }

  const rfqVendorsInclude = buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user);

  try {
    const [data, merchGroupData, whsecost] = await Promise.all([
      sql.RFQItem.findAll({
        order: order,
        subQuery: subQuery,
        attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES },
        as: 'RFQItem',
        where: condition,
        include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude),
      }),
      getCachedMerchGroup(sql),
      getCachedWhsePrice(sql),
    ]);

    // ... remap and sort ...
    items = items.sort(function (a, b) {
      return (a['Price Code'] == req.body.highlightedItem) ? -1 : 1;
    });

    result(null, items);
  } catch (err) {
    result(err, null);
  }
};
```

**Request body fields consumed:**
- `req.body.status` — 'archive' or null
- `req.body.divisionCode` — array of {divCode_code}
- `req.body.divisionStatus` — array of {RFQItemStatus_id}
- `req.body.filterd_rfq` — 'true' or null (vendor-only, filtered mode)
- `req.body.highlightedItem` — rfqItem_style_number to bump to top of sort

**Note:** No pagination (`limit`/`offset`) or sorting/filtering params are currently read or applied in SQL.

---

### 2. Include Builders and Eager Loads

#### buildRfqListLookupIncludes (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 109-159)

```javascript
function buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude) {
  return [
    rfqVendorsInclude,  // ← RFQVendor + nested Factory
    {
      model: sql.customers,
      as: 'customer',
      attributes: ['customers_id', 'customers_name', 'customers_code', 'customers_dilution', 'customers_logistic_load'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQItemStatus,
      as: 'RFQItemStatus',
      attributes: ['RFQItemStatus_id', 'RFQItemStatus_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.itemDepth,
      as: 'itemDepth',
      attributes: ['itemDepth_id', 'itemDepth_code', 'itemDepth_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.deliveryLocation,
      as: 'deliveryLocation',
      attributes: ['deliveryLocation_id', 'deliveryLocation_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQGroup,
      as: 'RFQGroup',
      attributes: ['RFQGroup_id', 'RFQGroup_name'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQStep,
      as: 'RFQStep',
      attributes: ['RFQStep_id', 'RFQStep_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [id, factory_name, factory_nickname, factory_status, factory_access, factory_country, sort_order]
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQContainer,
      as: 'RFQContainer',
      attributes: ['RFQContainer_id', 'RFQContainer_price'],
    },
  ];
}
```

#### buildRfqVendorsInclude (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 161-183)

```javascript
function buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user) {
  const include = {
    model: sql.RFQVendor,
    as: 'RFQVendors',
    required: false,
    attributes: RFQ_VENDOR_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [RFQVendor_id, RFQVendor_amount, RFQVendor_cbm_pc, RFQVendor_status, RFQitem_id_fk, RFQVendor_note, carton_*, req_status, vendor_id_fk, quote_date, std_vendor_id_fk, lead_time, price_terms, fob_country, fob_port, requote_requested, RFQVendor_archived, RFQVendor_archive_optout]
    include: [{
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      required: false,
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,
    }],
  };
  if (user.role === 'vendor') {
    include.where = { vendor_id_fk: vendorFilterIds... };
  }
  return include;
}
```

**Eager-loaded associations:**
- `RFQVendors` (left join, optional) → RFQVendor with nested Factory
- `customer` (left join)
- `RFQItemStatus` (left join)
- `itemDepth` (left join)
- `deliveryLocation` (left join)
- `RFQGroup` (left join)
- `RFQStep` (left join)
- `Factory` (left join) — "Chosen Vendor"
- `RFQContainer` (left join)

**Critical:** `subQuery: true` for internal roles (line 445). With 8 includes and a left-join-heavy structure, subQuery is necessary for LIMIT to work correctly (prevents row multiplication).

#### RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 5-17)

```javascript
const RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES = [
  'rfqItem_auditlog',
  'rfqItem_default_cbm',
  'rfqItem_gen_fob_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_whse_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_poe_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_mddp_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_ldp_margin',
  'rfqItem_cbm_per_piece',
  'rfqItem_quote_update',
  'rfqItem_price_per_cbm',
  'rfqItem_standardized_products',
];
```

---

### 3. remapRFQItem: Computed vs DB-Backed Fields

#### C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\utility.js, lines 19-286

**DIRECT DB COPIES (no computation):**
- 'Price Code' ← `rfqItem_style_number` (line 38)
- 'Notes' ← `rfqItem_notes` (line 51)
- 'Agent' ← `rfqItem_agent` (line 44, stores as global if set)
- 'License' ← `rfqItem_license` (line 67)
- 'Picture' ← `rfqItem_picturethumb` (line 68)
- 'Quantity' ← `rfqItem_quantity` (line 86)

- 'Assumed Wholesale' ← `rfqItem_wholesale` (line 104)
- 'Gen FOB Margin %' ← `rfqItem_gen_fob_margin` (line 106)
- 'Gen Whse Margin %' ← `rfqItem_gen_whse_margin` (line 107)
- 'Gen POE Margin %' ← `rfqItem_gen_poe_margin` (line 108)

- 'Gen POE Royalty %' ← `rfqItem_gen_poe_royalty` (line 121)
- 'Created Date' ← `rfqItem_created_date` (line 122)
- 'Date Modified' ← `rfqItem_date_modified` (line 123)
- 'FullSizePicture' ← `rfqItem_picture` (line 125)
- 'Gen FOB Sell Price' ← `rfqItem_gen_fob_sellprice` (line 129)
- 'Gen Whse Sell Price' ← `rfqItem_gen_whse_sellprice` (line 131)
- 'Gen POE Price for Sales' ← `rfqItem_gen_poe_pricesale` (line 139)
- 'Gen POE Sell Price' ← `rfqItem_gen_poe_sellprice` (line 140)
- 'CBM/pc' ← `rfqItem_cbm_per_price` (line 141)
- 'Gen MDDP Sell Price' ← `rfqItem_gen_mddp_sellprice` (line 145)

- 'Gen MDDP Margin %' ← `rfqItem_gen_mddp_margin` (line 147)
- 'Warehouse' ← `rfqItem_warehouse` (line 149)
- 'Freight $' ← `rfqItem_freight` (line 257)
- 'duty_rate_dollar_amount' ← `rfqItem_duty_rate_dollar_amount` (line 258)
- 'duty_rate_equation' ← `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (line 259)
- 'Container Price' ← `rfqItem_container_price` (line 260)
- 'copied_from_id' ← `rfqItem_copied_from_id` (line 261)
- 'Div' ← `rfqItem_divCode_id_fk` (line 210)
- All 'Lic*' margin/price/royalty fields (lines 170-188) ← direct DB columns
- All 'rfqItem_gen/lic_*_entered_*' fields (lines 190-206) ← direct DB columns

**COMPUTED IN JAVASCRIPT (NO DB COLUMN):**

**Numeric parse + assignment side effects:**
- 'FOB Cost' (line 78-85) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_fob_cost)`, stores in global `fobCost`
- 'Case Pack' (line 88-95) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_case_pack)`, stores in global `casePack`
- 'Dilution %' (line 71-77) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_dilution)`, stores in global `dilution`
- 'Duty Rate %' (line 96-102) — transforms to object `{ title: parseFloat(), duty_rate_dollar_amount: parseFloat() }`
- 'Logistics Load %' (line 132-138) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_logistic_load)`, stores in global `logisticsLoad`
- 'royalty' (line 59-65) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_royalty)`, stores in global `royalty`

**Object reconstruction from includes (PERSISTED, but assembled from relations):**
- 'Group' (line 39) ← `rfqItem_udf1` passed to `resolveMerchGroupField()` → object `{ title: mg_desc, mg_code, id }`
- 'Material' (line 40) ← `rfqItem_udf2` → merch group object
- 'Treatment' (line 41) ← `rfqItem_udf3` → merch group object
- 'Size (LxW)' (line 42) ← `rfqItem_size_l_w` → merch group object
- 'Construction' (line 43) ← `rfqItem_udf4` → merch group object
- 'Depth' (line 52) — from `itemDepth` include, assembled into `{ title, id, code }`
- 'Step' (line 53-57) — from `RFQStep` include, object with `title`, `id`, `factoriesStepUpdatedAt`
- 'Customer' (line 70) — from `customer` include, object with `title`, `id`, `code`, `dilution`, `logistics_load`
- 'RFQ Group' (line 105) — from `RFQGroup` include, object with `RFQGroup_name`, `RFQGroup_id`
- 'Status' (line 109) — from `RFQItemStatus` include, object with `title`, `id`
- 'Chosen Vendor' (line 112) — from `Factory` include, object with `title: factory_nickname`, `id`
- 'Delivery Location' (line 124) — from `deliveryLocation` include, object with `title`, `id`

**HIGHLY COMPUTED (complex logic, reference data merges):**
- 'Res' (line 150-169) — counts unique responded vs requested vendors from RFQVendors array: `{unique_responded.size}/{unique_requested.size}`. Iterates all RFQVendor rows, filters by `req_status !== 1`, checks if `RFQVendor_amount` is non-NaN. **Cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL without replicating this logic in a computed column or subquery.**
- 'Vendors' (line 211-256) — transforms full RFQVendors array into detail list of vendor objects. Sorts by price. Maps each to include Factory nickname, quote_date, lead_time, etc. **Cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL; entirely JS-side.**
- 'containercost' (line 37) — from `RFQContainer` include, transforms to object if present

**Reference data transforms (requires IN-MEMORY LOOKUP):**
- 'requested_price_cells' (lines 262-267) — `JSON.parse(rfqItem_requested_price_cells)` with fallback to `[]`
- 'price_sales_snapshots' (lines 268-273) — `JSON.parse(rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots)` with fallback to `{}`

### 4. RFQItem Model DB Columns (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\models\db\RFQItem.js)

Key grid columns mapped to DB columns:

| Grid Column | DB Column Name | Type | Sortable/Filterable in SQL |
|---|---|---|---|
| Price Code | `rfqItem_style_number` | STRING(255) | YES |
| Description | `rfqItem_description` | STRING(255) | YES |
| Status | `rfqItem_active` | INTEGER | YES (filter by ID only) |
| Date Modified | `rfqItem_date_modified` | DATE | YES |
| RFQ Group | `rfqItem_rfq_group` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK join) |
| Customer | `rfqItem_customer` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK join) |
| FOB Cost | `rfqItem_fob_cost` | STRING(255) | YES (stored as string, needs CAST to NUMERIC) |
| Landed Cost | `rfqItem_landed_cost` | STRING(255) | YES (string; persisted) |
| CBM/pc | `rfqItem_cbm_per_price` | STRING(255) | YES |
| Quantity | `rfqItem_quantity` | STRING(255) | YES |
| Created Date | `rfqItem_created_date` | DATE | YES |
| Factory (Chosen Vendor) | `rfqItem_choosen_vendor` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK join) |
| Size (LxW) | `rfqItem_size_l_w` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK to merch group) |
| Group (UDF1) | `rfqItem_udf1` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK to merch group) |
| Material (UDF2) | `rfqItem_udf2` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK to merch group) |
| Treatment (UDF3) | `rfqItem_udf3` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK to merch group) |
| Construction (UDF4) | `rfqItem_udf4` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK to merch group) |
| Depth | `rfqItem_delivery_loc` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK join) |
| Step | `rfqItem_step` | INTEGER | INDIRECT (FK join) |
| **Res (responded/requested count)** | **NONE — computed in JS** | — | **NO, must compute after fetch** |

**Special columns:**
- `rfqItem_is_landed_cost_manual` (BOOLEAN) — marks whether landed cost is user-entered vs computed
- `rfqItem_archive` (INTEGER) — global archive flag
- `rfqItem_rfq_group` (INTEGER) — FK to RFQGroup
- `rfqItem_divCode_id_fk` (STRING) — division code (filter only, no sort)

### 5. Routing: /getAllItem to Customer.getAll

**C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\routes\lib.router.js, line 15:**
```javascript
router.post("/getAllItem/", authRole([...]), customers.findAll);
```

**C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\controllers\main.controller.js, lines 30-35:**
```javascript
exports.findAll = (req, res) => {
  Customer.getAll(req, (err, data) => {
    if (err) return sendError(res, err, 'SYS-002');
    res.json(data);
  });
};
```

**BFF routing (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-bff\src\serviceMap.js):**
```javascript
const SERVICE_MAP = {
    core: process.env.CORE_BACKEND_URL,  // ← designflow-backend /api/core/*
    item_master: ...,
    tracking: ...,
    data_sync: ...
};
```

BFF proxy (`designflow-bff/src/backendProxy.js`) forwards `/api/core/*` to `CORE_BACKEND_URL` (designflow-backend) with OIDC identity token.

### 6. Pagination/Sort/Filter Translation Helpers

**None exist currently.** The codebase has:
- **Line 445 in lib.model.js:** `subQuery = true` for internal roles only (Sequelize workaround for LIMIT with joins)
- **Line 415, 427, 444:** `Sequelize.literal()` raw SQL order clauses
- **No offset/limit:** All rows returned in memory, then sorted by JS (line 474-476)

**Potential reuse patterns found:**
- `rfqListQuery.js` helper already encapsulates `buildRfqListLookupIncludes()`, `buildRfqVendorsInclude()`, and cache functions
- Test suite (`tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`) demonstrates builder patterns

## SUMMARY TABLE: Computed vs DB-Backed (Key Grid Columns)

| Column | DB Column | Computed in JS | Sortable in SQL | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price Code | rfqItem_style_number | No | YES | Direct string |
| Description | rfqItem_description | No | YES | Direct string |
| Status | rfqItem_active (ID) | Partial | YES (ID only) | ID stored in DB, title from join |
| Date Modified | rfqItem_date_modified | No | YES | Direct DATE |
| Created Date | rfqItem_created_date | No | YES | Direct DATE |
| RFQ Group | rfqItem_rfq_group | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, name from RFQGroup join |
| Customer | rfqItem_customer | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, details from customer join |
| FOB Cost | rfqItem_fob_cost | Partial | YES (requires CAST) | Stored as STRING, needs numeric cast to sort |
| Landed Cost | rfqItem_landed_cost | No | YES | Stored as STRING, persisted |
| CBM/pc | rfqItem_cbm_per_price | No | YES | Stored as STRING |
| Factory (Chosen Vendor) | rfqItem_choosen_vendor | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, name from Factory join |
| **Res (responded/requested)** | **NONE** | **YES, fully** | **NO** | Iterates RFQVendors, counts non-null prices vs req_status. Must compute post-fetch. |
| **Group** | rfqItem_udf1 | Yes | INDIRECT | Merch group lookup. Stored as merch group ID, but display name requires in-memory map lookup. |
| **Material** | rfqItem_udf2 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Treatment** | rfqItem_udf3 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Construction** | rfqItem_udf4 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Size (LxW)** | rfqItem_size_l_w | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group — merch group lookup. |
| Depth | rfqItem_delivery_loc | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, title from itemDepth join |
| Step | rfqItem_step | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, title from RFQStep join |
